question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 18.00% on 2026-03-28 for the Metaculus question "Will Saudi Arabia and Israel agree to normalise diplomatic relations during 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on Saudi-Israel normalization will rise above 18.00% by March 28. The CP dropped from 21% to 18% between March 7-14 as the US-Israel war on Iran erupted (Feb 28). Since the last CP reading, the situation has dramatically worsened: Iranian missiles struck Riyadh during a ministerial meeting (March 18-19), Saudi Arabia warned of military action against Iran, and multiple credible analysts explicitly state normalization is "effectively nonexistent." The CP sits exactly at the threshold, and resolution requires strictly above 18.00%. With strong downward pressure from escalating conflict and no positive normalization signals, the CP is more likely to stay at or drop below 18% than to rise above it. Monte Carlo simulation with negatively-skewed drift yields ~22% probability of crossing upward.

factors.description: CP dropped from 21% to 18% (3pp) in one week (March 7-14), coinciding with US-Israel war on Iran starting Feb 28. Strong downward momentum.

Since March 14, news has further escalated - Iranian missiles hitting Riyadh during ministerial meeting (March 18-19), Saudi Arabia warning of military action against Iran. Should push CP further down.
Multiple credible analysts explicitly state normalization is 'effectively nonexistent' (JPost) and 'unrealistic' (NZZ). Saudi officials view Israel as 'aggressive hegemon'.
CP is exactly at the threshold (18.00%). Resolution requires STRICTLY above 18.00%. Even staying flat resolves NO - asymmetry favors NO when drift is negative.
726 forecasters on underlying question - large base provides stability, hard for random noise to push above threshold.
Counterargument: some could argue shared Iran threat brings Saudi-Israel closer, contrarian forecasters/bots might push CP up. 9 months remain in 2026.
Recency-weighted median means recent forecasts have outsized influence. A few new higher-value forecasts could shift CP up, introducing randomness.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 23% probability (YES)**

The CP on Saudi-Israel normalization sits exactly at the 18.00% threshold and must be **strictly above** it on March 28 to resolve YES. Key factors driving my estimate:

- **Strong downward momentum**: CP fell from 21% → 18% in one week as the US-Israel war on Iran erupted Feb 28
- **Worsening news since last reading**: Iranian missiles hit Riyadh during a ministerial meeting on March 18-19; Saudi Arabia warns of military action against Iran; trust in Tehran has "completely collapsed"
- **Analysts say normalization is off the table**: Multiple credible sources (JPost, NZZ, CFR) explicitly state normalization is "effectively nonexistent" or "unrealistic"
- **Threshold asymmetry**: CP must be strictly > 18.00% — even staying flat means NO

The ~23% YES probability reflects that while the dominant pressure is clearly downward, CP movements have some randomness from individual forecaster activity and the recency-weighted median methodology.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41087) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-28 16:50:13 is higher than 18.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 18.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-28 16:50:13, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
